steps.title: Spot the contrast
steps.content: The passage sets up an expectation that the spiders' spread is due to aggression, then reveals they are actually gentle giants that freeze when disturbed.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contrast
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast: researchers expected aggression to explain the spiders' spread, but discovered the opposite — the spiders are gentle and freeze when disturbed. The blank needs a transition signaling reversal of expectation.
  • Choice A — In other words,: signals restatement or clarification, so fails — the second clause is not a rephrasing of the first. ✗
  • Choice B — Therefore,: signals cause-and-effect, so fails — the gentleness is not a result of the researchers' expectation. ✗
  • Choice C — For example,: signals a specific instance of a general claim, so fails — the finding contradicts, not illustrates, the hypothesis. ✗
  • Choice D — Instead,: signals reversal or substitution, so fits — the spiders behave opposite to what was expected. ✓
D is correct.
